prompt: Draft a detailed protein crystallisation protocol for human thrombin (alpha-thrombin) in complex with a small-molecule inhibitor candidate for structure-based drug design. The protocol should include: (1) protein preparation: human thrombin (final concentration 10 mg/mL) in 20 mM sodium phosphate pH 7.0, 150 mM NaCl, (2) inhibitor soaking: incubate thrombin with 2-fold molar excess inhibitor dissolved in DMSO (≤5% final DMSO), (3) crystallisation screen: Hampton Research Crystal Screen I/II in 96-well sitting-drop format, 0.2 µL protein + 0.2 µL reservoir, (4) optimisation: PEG 4000/NaCl grid screen at pH 6.5–8.0, (5) cryoprotection: step-wise addition of 25% glycerol before flash-cooling in liquid nitrogen, (6) data collection strategy: target 1.8 Å resolution at synchrotron (Cu Kα backup), (7) expected crystal form: monoclinic P21 with 2 molecules per AU. Include troubleshooting for crystal twinning and inhibitor occupancy verification.
source_citation: Derived from uniprot (2024_05) and protein crystallography methodology literature; target: human thrombin; organism: Homo sapiens; catalogue version v1.0

_provenance.target_class: serine protease coagulation factor (drug target crystallography)
